etimes“It hurts to close kitchens…”: Delhi restaurant shuts amid LPG crisis; owner claims ₹5,000 black market rateFood businesses across India face disruption due to an ongoing LPG shortage. In South Delhi, the North Indian restaurant Tadka Rani has temporarily ceased operations. Owner Gagandeep Singh Sapra cited the unavailability of LPG cylinders and exorbitant black market prices. He expressed distress over closing kitchens and taking the restaurant offline from delivery platforms.

etimesYoung girl’s viral video shows phone exploding in her face – but is it real? Here's all we knowA shocking video from China shows a young girl's phone exploding while charging, sending her flying. The incident has ignited widespread concern online about device safety, with many users sharing fears and advising against using phones while plugged in. Experts emphasize using quality chargers and monitoring battery health to prevent such dangerous overheating.

etimesLPG shortage hits restaurants: Bengaluru eatery shuts days after opening; owner says, “My heart is broken"Restaurants in major Indian cities are struggling with a severe LPG shortage. This gas supply issue is impacting kitchen operations, forcing some establishments to temporarily shut down. One newly opened Bengaluru restaurant announced a pause in service due to the crisis. The situation highlights the vulnerability of businesses to fuel supply disruptions.
